title: Spicy Sticky Korean Beef Stir-fry
description: A sweet, velvety dish featuring tabasco sauce and whole30-friendly spices, perfect for a lazy Sunday dinner.
instructions: Slice the beef thinly against the grain.
Finely chop the onion and garlic.
Heat oil in a wok or large frying pan over medium-high heat. Add chopped onions, cook until translucent.
Add minced garlic and stir for about 30 seconds.
Throw in sliced beef, salt to taste, and cook for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Mix together Korean Chili Paste, Tabasco Sauce, Fish Sauce, Soy Sauce, and Brown Sugar. Slowly pour the mixture over the meat.
Pour in water and stir well until it starts boiling.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and let simmer for about 10 minutes or until the beef is tender and sauce has thickened slightly.
Slice scallions thinly if using, and add them along with any desired green onions at the last minute. Stir gently and cook for another minute.
